campus: Genoa (Department of Computer Science, Bioengineering, Robotics and Systems Engineering - DIBRIS)
admission_type: open access (free access) with mandatory verification of curricular requirements and assessment of adequacy of personal preparation; no competitive entrance test. Non-EU applicants residing abroad must pass UniGeApply pre-evaluation

academic_requirements.summary: A first-cycle (bachelor's) degree or recognised equivalent, with specific curricular credits in information-engineering disciplines and a minimum weighted grade average; no written or oral entrance test (documentary assessment only).
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A bachelor's degree (laurea) or equivalent, in particular the Information Engineering class L-8 (D.M. 270/2004) or an equivalent foreign degree. Direct-admission for University of Genoa graduates in Biomedical, Electronic, Computer or Telecommunications Engineering.
academic_requirements.required_credits_cfu: At least 36 CFU (or equivalent knowledge) in the scientific-disciplinary sectors (SSD) of the basic-training activities of class L-8 Information Engineering, and at least 45 CFU (or equivalent knowledge) in the characterising sectors ING-INF/01-06 (Automation, Bioengineering, Electronics, Computer Engineering, Telecommunications).
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: Background in biomedical engineering or other industrial/information engineering fields; also open to graduates from mathematical, physical and natural sciences who meet the curricular credit prerequisites.
academic_requirements.minimum_gpa_or_threshold: A CFU-weighted grade average of at least 22.50/30, equivalently a Cumulative Grade Point Average (CGPA) of at least 75% of the maximum obtainable. Applicants below 22.50/30 cannot enrol.
academic_requirements.international_qualification: Holders of foreign qualifications are assessed for equivalence; non-EU candidates residing abroad apply through the UniGeApply pre-evaluation portal (which lists a 75% minimum CGPA and an engineering-bachelor requirement for this master).

accepted_proofs: A B2-level English certificate from a recognised body.
A degree or master's degree taught in English (medium of instruction).
A B2-level English examination passed within a degree course.
The free CLAT B2 English test offered by the University of Genoa to prospective Bioengineering students.
exemption_rules: Holding a degree taught in English (MOI certificate) or having passed a B2 English exam within a degree course satisfies the requirement without a separate certificate.
language_requirements.assessment_or_upload_stage: English proficiency is verified at the assessment / application stage; non-EU applicants upload the certificate (or MOI) on UniGeApply, and candidates without proof may sit the free CLAT B2 test. Holders of foreign qualifications may also be subject to an Italian-language check with supplementary activities assigned on failure.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: Verification (assessment) of adequacy of personal preparation - documentary, not a TOLC or standardized test.
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: Applicants to the master's degree, to confirm adequate preparation against the curricular and grade requirements.
entry_exam_or_test.format: No interview or written/oral entrance test. Assessment is based on review of the academic record (transcript with weighted average), the CV, and English-language certification.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: Conducted through the documentary procedure; non-EU applicants are assessed via UniGeApply (no remote interview/test required).
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: Preparation is adequate if the weighted grade average is at least 22.50/30 (CGPA at least 75% of the maximum); applicants below this threshold are not admitted.

rounds: Spring session for EU citizens and non-EU residents in Italy (UniGeApply): opens 9 April 2026; master's degrees close 28 August 2026.
application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: Local pre-enrolment/online enrolment on UniGe online services generally opens in April each year and follows the UniGe enrolment calendar after a positive assessment. [uncertain]
application_deadline_eu.notes: EU/resident applicants are assessed via the documentary verification of preparation and are not competitively ranked. The course page does not publish a separate programme-specific first-year EU application deadline, so the institution-wide UniGeApply spring window is used.

rounds: Fall session for non-EU candidates requiring a visa (UniGeApply): opens 26 November 2025; institution-wide close 20 March 2026; pre-evaluation results by 22 May 2026.
application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: After a positive UniGeApply pre-evaluation, visa applicants complete Universitaly pre-enrolment (Universitaly window 4 May 2026 to 19 June 2026) and then apply for the study visa.
application_deadline_non_eu.early_closure: No programme-specific early closure was found for Bioengineering in the official sources reviewed. [uncertain]
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: Non-EU visa applicants must use the fall session and the UniGeApply portal; an applicant may submit up to two assessment requests.

required_documents.notes: Mandatory for all UniGeApply master's applicants; reviewed in the assessment of preparation.
Proof of the prior first-cycle qualification; for the engineering pathway, evidence of the relevant degree class (e.g. L-8).

required_documents.document_name: Transcript of records (list of exams with credits and grades, including the weighted average / CGPA)
required_documents.notes: Used to verify the 36+45 CFU curricular prerequisites and the 22.50/30 (75% CGPA) minimum.

required_documents.document_name: English language proficiency certificate (B2) or Medium-of-Instruction (MOI) certificate
required_documents.notes: B2 certificate, English-taught degree, B2 exam in a degree course, or the free CLAT B2 test.
Passport is the only accepted identity document for non-EU applicants on UniGeApply (photo as JPG, supporting documents as PDF up to 2 MB).

source_quotes.quote: Bioengineering - Teaching language English - Class LM-21 R (Biomedical Engineering) - Genova, 120 CFU

aimed at graduates from biomedical engineering or other fields of industrial and information engineering, but is also open to graduates from the various fields of mathematical, physical and natural sciences

at least 36 CFU ... in the scientific disciplinary fields (SSD) indicated for the basic training activities provided by the Degrees of Class L-8 ... at least 45 CFUs, or equivalent knowledge ... ING-INF/01-06
To be sufficient in the assessment you must have a cfu-weighted average of 22.50 or higher. Students with a lower average will not be admitted.
B2 certificate; a degree/master's degree taught in English; a B2-level English language examination passed in a degree course
Bioengineering - Minimum CGPA: 75% - Remote interview/test: No - Only Bachelor in the field of engineering

uncertainty_notes: Teaching language confirmed English and course code 11933 confirmed on the official corsi.unige.it page; this file replaces an earlier flat-string version with the mandatory rich object/array structure.
The 75% CGPA threshold matches both the official requisites page (22.5/30 = 75%) and the UniGeApply summary; UniGeApply also states no remote interview/test and an engineering-bachelor requirement.
No programme-specific fall-session early closure was found for Bioengineering; institution-wide UniGeApply windows were used for the application deadlines.

First-year EU application deadline reflects the institution-wide UniGeApply spring window; the course page only lists the generic enrolment calendar, not a programme-specific first-year EU application deadline.
